title: Update on the Progress of the Parks Service Plan Development
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: April 15, 2026
TO: Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Nathan Ullyot, Parks and Recreation Director
Karina Mercier, Project Manager – Park Planner
DEPT: Parks and Recreation
SUBJECT: Update on the Progress of the Parks Service Plan Development
This memo is intended to provide Mayor and City Council an update on the development of the Parks Service Plan to investigate the feasibility of a Park District that was approved via a professional services agreement with PROS Consulting, Inc on June 4, 2025. The professional services agreement with a total cost of $223,550 has been offset by contributions from Washoe County in the amount of $100,000 and from the Redevelopment Agency in the amount of $60,000 which have been formally received by the Parks and Recreation Department.

Since Mayor and City Council approval of the professional services agreement, City Parks staff has maintained regular meetings with Washoe County and City of Sparks staff. The scope of collaboration has since broadened to include representatives from the Sun Valley General Improvement District. PROS Consulting has also held stakeholder interviews with representatives of the Reno-Sparks Indian Colony. Together with PROS Consulting the team has completed the initial stakeholder interviews, data requests, preliminary site visits and facility assessments. The feedback received from elected officials and stakeholders is that there is a strong potential value in a regional park district, but that support for a ballot initiative depends on credible, transparent communication that demonstrates tangible, jurisdiction-specific benefits.

Survey
A key focus in this stage of the Parks Service Plan's development has been surveying. PROS Consulting has completed the survey, which was mailed directly to pre-selected residents, ensuring that results accurately reflect the demographics of our region. The survey required a minimum of 600 responses from the pre-selected groups. Of the minimum responses required, 300 were needed from Reno, 150 from unincorporated Washoe County, and 150 from Sparks. At the completion of the process there were a total of 748 surveys collected utilizing random sampling to ensure statistically significant and valid results that represent the region's population.

Staff from both the Cities and County also started an additional community survey to ensure all residents could participate. This accessible version was offered in Spanish as well. Alongside the community survey, City and County staff created a joint landing page at OurParksNV.org that explains what a Service Plan is and outlines the possible benefits of a new park district. The community survey ended on March 13, 2026.

Next Steps
The PROS Consulting team has provided a draft Levels of Service analysis which is being reviewed and verified by each jurisdiction. Additionally, PROS Consulting is working to analyze the feedback received through the surveys. PROS Consulting will present the survey findings and the status of the Parks Service Plan at a concurrent meeting with all the jurisdiction's Recreation and Parks boards which is tentatively scheduled for April 30, 2026. Councils/Commissions are invited to attend, and a memo will be sent to Mayor and City Council summarizing the April 30, 2026 meeting's content.

The Redevelopment Agency Advisory Board (RAAB) has also requested an update from Parks and Recreation Department staff. Parks and Recreation Department staff will present along with PROS Consulting at the May 4 RAAB meeting.

The Parks Service Plan is on track for completion by August 2026. Should Mayor and City Council desire more detail regarding the Parks Service Plan tasks and timeline, Parks and Recreation Department staff can provide the project schedule.

fields.authors: Nathan Ullyot, Parks and Recreation Director; Karina Mercier, Project Manager – Park Planner
fields.tldr: The Parks and Recreation Department is updating Mayor and City Council on the development of a Parks Service Plan investigating the feasibility of a regional Park District, contracted with PROS Consulting, Inc. The $223,550 professional services agreement has been partially offset by $100,000 from Washoe County and $60,000 from the Redevelopment Agency. Key milestones completed include stakeholder interviews, facility assessments, and a statistically valid survey of 748 respondents across the region. The plan is on track for completion by August 2026, with upcoming presentations to parks boards on April 30 and the RAAB on May 4.
fields.key_stats: Total contract cost: $223,550; Washoe County contribution: $100,000; Redevelopment Agency contribution: $60,000; Survey minimum required: 600 responses (300 Reno, 150 unincorporated Washoe County, 150 Sparks); Total surveys collected: 748; Community survey end date: March 13, 2026
fields.key_decisions: Professional services agreement with PROS Consulting, Inc. was approved by Mayor and City Council on June 4, 2025. Collaboration scope was broadened to include the Sun Valley General Improvement District. Stakeholder feedback indicates strong potential value in a regional park district, with support for a ballot initiative contingent on credible, transparent, jurisdiction-specific communication.
fields.action_items: PROS Consulting to present survey findings and Parks Service Plan status at a concurrent parks/recreation boards meeting tentatively scheduled for April 30, 2026; Parks and Recreation staff to send a summary memo to Mayor and City Council following the April 30 meeting; Parks and Recreation staff and PROS Consulting to present to the Redevelopment Agency Advisory Board (RAAB) on May 4, 2026; Parks Service Plan to be completed by August 2026; Staff can provide the full project schedule to Council upon request.

dollar_amounts: $223,550 – total cost of professional services agreement with PROS Consulting, Inc.
dates: June 4, 2025 – approval of professional services agreement with PROS Consulting, Inc.
April 30, 2026 – tentative concurrent parks/recreation boards meeting with PROS Consulting presentation
August 2026 – target completion date for Parks Service Plan
people: Nathan Ullyot, Parks and Recreation Director
Karina Mercier, Project Manager – Park Planner
